diagrams.title: Disk Geometry Structure
diagrams.description: Shows physical organization of a magnetic hard disk with multiple platters, tracks, sectors, and cylinders
elements: Cylinder (tracks at same radius across all surfaces)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how data is physically organized and accessed on magnetic disks, and why cylinder concept matters for performance

diagrams.title: Disk Access Time Components
diagrams.description: Breakdown of disk access time showing seek time, rotational latency, and transfer time
elements: Rotational latency (waiting for sector to rotate)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why secondary storage is slow compared to RAM, and how disk scheduling algorithms aim to minimize these delays

diagrams.title: RAID Levels Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of different RAID levels showing data and parity distribution
elements: RAID 0 - data striped across disks (no redundancy)
RAID 5 - data striped with distributed parity
diagrams.purpose: Understanding trade-offs between performance, storage efficiency, and fault tolerance in storage systems
